sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Jerry, a 45-year-old male, presented to the office with an ankle injury sustained during a basketball game with his son.
He reported that he landed awkwardly and twisted his ankle, resulting in pain and swelling on the outside of the ankle.
He has been experiencing difficulty walking and feeling instability in the ankle.
He recently restarted working out and was concerned about disrupting his routine.
He also mentioned that his son is doing well and is enthusiastic about playing basketball.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's ankle was swollen on the lateral side, with exquisite tenderness to palpation laterally and some tenderness over the medial deltoid region.
The skin was intact, and capillary refill was brisk.
The ankle did not appear to be malaligned.
Test Results: The patient had an X-ray of the right ankle, which showed a displaced lateral malleolus fracture at the Weber C level, with no evidence of medial or posterior malleolar fractures.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a displaced lateral malleolar fracture, which is likely to require surgery to stabilize and promote healing.
Problem list: Lateral malleolar fracture; status: confirmed
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg orally twice daily to reduce swelling and pain.
I advised the patient to ice the ankle and elevate it regularly.
I will obtain crutches to aid in ambulation.
The patient will need to undergo surgery to stabilize the fracture, which will likely require a plate and screws.
I recommended that the patient continue coaching his son's basketball team, as the fracture will likely require a 3-month recovery period.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me in 3 months to assess the healing of the fracture and plan for further rehabilitation.
